calendar.matters:
Petition For Writ Of Mandate (Part 1 Of 4)
Off Calendar May-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Chris Morales, Esq. 415-552-1215 of Morales Law Firm for Petitioner Luis Camacho who is also present.
John McGlothin, Deputy Attorney General 510-879-0295 for Respondent Dept. of Motor Vehicles.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court inquires re: the four issues raised on the writ - form, admonishment, Phlebotomist, Title 17 CCR 1219.3. Both sides Argue. Respondent cites 1221.2. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER LOUIS CAMACHO's Petition For Writ Of Mandate.

Taken under Submission.* Respondent advises the Court he does not need to retrieve the Copy of the Administrative record lodged with the Court. It can be recycled. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano , CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)

*After the hearing, the Court prepares and signs an Order Denying Petition for Writ of Mandate. (see scanned order for details)*

Petition For Writ Of Mandate (Part 1 Of 4)
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Continued To May-04-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER LOUIS CAMACHO's Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate is continued to May 4, 2018 per the request of the moving party.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.
